content.problemStatement: In this task, we will create a program that formats an email address by removing unnecessary spaces. This helps practice basic string methods like `.strip()` and `.replace() for cleaning user input.
constraints: The string may contain any visible ASCII characters and spaces
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a raw email address string (length ≤ 100) which may include spaces at any position.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output a line in the exact format:
Formatted email: <formatted_email>
where every space character in the original string is replaced by an underscore ('_').

sampleTestCases.output:
Formatted email: john.doe_@example.com
Formatted email: jane_doe123_@gmail.com
sampleTestCases.explanation: Each space in the raw email is replaced by an underscore.
Leading and trailing spaces become leading and trailing underscores.
Every space, including consecutive ones, is turned into an underscore.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line (including leading and trailing spaces). Iterate over the characters and replace every space character with an underscore. Output the transformed string prefixed by the required label. The algorithm is linear in the total number of characters across all test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of input) – each character is inspected once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input buffer – transformation is done in‑place or with a new string of the same size.
